issueDetails.example:
"The endpoint appears to be vulnerable to Server Side Request Forgery attack. The original request was replayed by replacing CSV upload parameter with url that redirects to AWS IMDS endpoint. The application responded with 2XX success code and also gave out sensitive AWS information in response. Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF) flaws occur whenever an API is fetching a remote resource without validating the user-supplied URL. It allows an attacker to coerce the application to send a crafted request to an unexpected destination, even when protected by a firewall or a VPN.
 "Modern concepts in application development make SSRF more common and more dangerous.
" "More common - the following concepts encourage developers to access an external resource based on user input: Webhooks, file fetching from URLs, custom SSO, and URL previews.
" "More dangerous - Modern technologies like cloud providers, Kubernetes, and Docker expose management and control channels over HTTP on predictable, well-known paths. Those channels are an easy target for an SSRF attack.
" "It is also more challenging to limit outbound traffic from your application, because of the connected nature of modern applications.
" + "The SSRF risk can not always be completely eliminated. While choosing a protection mechanism, it is important to consider the business risks and needs."

issueDescription.example: Quite often, APIs do not impose any restrictions on the size or number of resources that can be requested by the user.

apiCollectionIds.description: List of API collection IDs this role has access to.

apiCollectionIds.description: Updated list of API collection IDs this role has access to.

defaultInviteRole.description: Whether this role should become the default role for new invites.

allowedFeaturesForUser.description: Updated list of feature keys enabled for users in this role.

/api/fetchDefaultPayload.description: Fetches the default payload configuration for a specific domain.

domain.description: Domain name to fetch the default payload for.

200.description: Returns the default payload configuration for the domain.

defaultPayload.description: DefaultPayload document for the specified domain.

schema.example: application/json
YOUR_API_KEY from Settings > Integrations > Akto APIs

/api/fetchAllDefaultPayloads.description: Fetches all default payload configurations saved for the current account.

200.description: Returns all default payloads for the account.

domains.description: List of DefaultPayload objects, one per configured domain.

schema.example: application/json
YOUR_API_KEY from Settings > Integrations > Akto APIs

/api/fetchGlobalVars.description: Fetches saved global host variable mappings and known hosts for the specified collections.

apiCollectionIds.description: Collection IDs whose hosts should be included in the response.

200.description: Returns current host mappings and collection hosts.

modifyHostDetails.description: Saved host modification mappings plus hosts extracted from the specified collections.

schema.example: application/json
YOUR_API_KEY from Settings > Integrations > Akto APIs

/api/fetchTestCollectionConfiguration.description: Fetches test configuration properties (headers, cookies, auth requirements, etc.) for a specific API collection.

apiCollectionId.description: ID of the API collection to fetch configuration for.

200.description: Returns the test collection configuration properties.

testCollectionProperties.description: List of TestCollectionProperty objects for the collection.

YOUR_API_KEY from Settings > Integrations > Akto APIs

/api/fetchPropertyIds.description: Fetches all available test collection property types with their metadata (id, title, type, impacted test categories).

propertyIds.description: Map of property enum name to its metadata object.

schema.example: application/json
YOUR_API_KEY from Settings > Integrations > Akto APIs

/api/buildDependencyTable.description: Builds a paginated dependency flow table with API nodes, their parameters (from traffic and source code), and replacement details.

apiCollectionIds.description: Collection IDs to build the dependency table for.

skip.description: Number of records to skip for pagination.

200.description: Returns the dependency table with nodes, parameters, and replacement details.

dependencyTableList.description: Paginated list of dependency nodes with parameter data.

replaceDetails.description: Replacement detail configurations for the collections.

schema.example: application/json
YOUR_API_KEY from Settings > Integrations > Akto APIs

/api/modifyTestingRunConfig.description: Modifies an existing testing run configuration including test suites, subcategories, schedule, concurrency, alerts, role, app URL override, and auto-ticketing settings.

testingRunConfigId.description: ID of the testing run config to modify.

editableTestingRunConfig.description: Updated configuration fields for the testing run.

autoTicketingDetails.description: Optional auto-ticketing configuration (e.g., Jira project settings).

schema.example: application/json
YOUR_API_KEY from Settings > Integrations > Akto APIs

/api/addAuthMechanism.description: Adds a new authentication mechanism (hardcoded token, login flow, sample data, or TLS) for API testing.

type.description: Authentication type. One of: HARDCODED, LOGIN_REQUEST, SAMPLE_DATA, TLS_AUTH.

authParamData.description: List of auth parameter entries specifying where and how to inject credentials.

requestData.description: HTTP request steps used to obtain credentials (for LOGIN_REQUEST type).

schema.example: application/json
YOUR_API_KEY from Settings > Integrations > Akto APIs

/api/triggerLoginSteps.description: Executes login flow steps and returns step-by-step responses. Used to validate login sequences before saving an auth mechanism.

authParamData.description: Auth parameters extracted from login flow responses.

requestData.description: Ordered list of HTTP requests forming the login flow.

miniTestingServiceName.description: Optional name of mini-testing service to execute the login steps.

200.description: Returns responses for each login flow step.

responses.description: Step-by-step HTTP responses from executing the login flow.

schema.example: application/json
YOUR_API_KEY from Settings > Integrations > Akto APIs

/api/createTestSuite.description: Creates a new custom test suite with a given name and list of test subcategories.

testSuiteName.description: Display name for the new test suite. A numeric suffix is appended if the name already exists.

subCategoryList.description: List of test subcategory identifiers to include in this suite.

schema.example: application/json
YOUR_API_KEY from Settings > Integrations > Akto APIs

/api/modifyTestSuite.description: Updates an existing custom test suite's name and/or subcategories. Only the creator or an ADMIN can modify a suite.

testSuiteHexId.description: Hex-encoded ObjectId of the test suite to modify.

testSuiteName.description: New display name for the test suite.

subCategoryList.description: Replacement list of test subcategory identifiers.

YOUR_API_KEY from Settings > Integrations > Akto APIs

YOUR_API_KEY from Settings > Integrations > Akto APIs

severityToBeUpdated.description: New severity level. One of: CRITICAL, HIGH, MEDIUM, LOW.

200.description: Severity updated across all related collections.

/api/getMcpServersByAgent.description: Retrieves MCP servers configured for a specific agent or device, including server name, URL, and last seen timestamp.

agentId.description: Optional agent ID to filter MCP servers by.

200.description: Returns MCP servers for the specified agent/device.

YOUR_API_KEY from Settings > Integrations > Akto APIs

/api/getAgentLogs.description: Fetches agent logs for a specific agent within a time range, sorted by timestamp (newest first), capped at 10,000 logs.

agentId.description: Required agent ID to fetch logs for.

startTime.description: Optional start timestamp in seconds (defaults to 24 hours ago).

endTime.description: Optional end timestamp in seconds (defaults to current time).

200.description: Returns agent logs for the specified time range.

agentLogs.description: List of agent log entries, sorted by timestamp (newest first).

schema.example: application/json
YOUR_API_KEY from Settings > Integrations > Akto APIs

/api/getMcpdata.description: Fetches MCP-related metrics and data based on filter type, including API counts, audit alerts, and traffic analysis.

filterType.description: Type of filter. One of: TOTAL_APIS, NEW_APIS_7_DAYS, THIRD_PARTY_APIS, RECENT_OPEN_ALERTS, CRITICAL_APIS, TOOLS, PROMPTS, RESOURCES, MCP_SERVER, TOP_3_APPLICATIONS_BY_TRAFFIC, POLICY_GUARDRAIL_APIS.

200.description: Returns MCP data matching the filter type.

mcpDataCount.description: Count of data matching the filter type.

auditAlerts.description: List of McpAuditInfo (only for RECENT_OPEN_ALERTS filter).

response.description: Additional response data (alertDetails or topApplications depending on filter type).

schema.example: application/json
YOUR_API_KEY from Settings > Integrations > Akto APIs

mcpRegistryConfig.description: Current MCP registry configuration with all registries.
